active_ingredients: Esomeprazole magnesium 20 mg delayed-release capsule/tablet (OTC, e.g., Nexium 24HR); 20 mg and 40 mg by prescription
indications_and_usage: Uses (OTC): treats frequent heartburn (2 or more days a week). It is a proton pump inhibitor closely related to omeprazole; not for immediate relief and may take 1-4 days for full effect. By prescription it is used for GERD with erosive esophagitis, NSAID-associated ulcer risk reduction, H. pylori eradication, and hypersecretory conditions.
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to esomeprazole or substituted benzimidazole PPIs; do not use with rilpivirine-containing products; do not use with nelfinavir.
warnings_and_cautions: Same PPI cautions as omeprazole: not for immediate relief; see a doctor for alarm symptoms (trouble swallowing, GI bleeding, weight loss, chest pain); long-term use risks - fractures, low magnesium, B12 deficiency, C. difficile, acute interstitial nephritis, lupus erythematosus. Use the lowest dose for the shortest time.
adverse_reactions: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, flatulence, abdominal pain, constipation, dry mouth; long-term metabolic and infection risks as with other PPIs.
drug_interactions: Like omeprazole, may reduce clopidogrel activation (use caution / prefer an alternative). Reduces absorption of acid-dependent drugs (ketoconazole, itraconazole, atazanavir, erlotinib, iron). May raise methotrexate, tacrolimus, citalopram, and digoxin levels. Contraindicated with rilpivirine and nelfinavir; reduces nelfinavir/atazanavir exposure.
dosage_and_administration: Adults 18 years and over (OTC): one 20 mg capsule/tablet daily, in the morning before eating, for 14 days; do not use for more than 14 days or more often than every 4 months unless directed by a doctor. By prescription, GERD is 20-40 mg once daily. Children under 18: ask a doctor.
overdosage: Overdose symptoms have been mild (drowsiness, confusion, blurred vision, tachycardia, nausea, headache, dry mouth); supportive care. Contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
